input: Miss Kobayashi's Dragon Maid - S00E31 [A Lonely Dragon Wants to be Loved].mkv
note: TRACKED KNOWN FAILURE (in tests/known_failures/self_rolled.txt). A real file from the neoDESU bundle's Specials folder. The bracketed phrase is the episode title, but release_group runs before episode_title and greedily claims the only trailing bracket, so we emit release_group instead. `Title - NN [X]` is structurally identical whether X is a group (`[Taka]`) or an episode title, and word-count heuristics regress real multi-word groups (`[bodlerov & torrents ru]`), so this is left as the recorded-correct-but-unreached target rather than fixed by overfitting.

output.title: Miss Kobayashi's Dragon Maid
output.episode_title: A Lonely Dragon Wants to be Loved

note: github.com/erengy/anitomy issue #37 -- colon between an episode marker and the episode title wasn't a recognized delimiter, so the whole filename fell back to being the title. Fixed by adding ':' to detail/delimiter.rs's is_delimiter (verified against upstream's develop/master headers: neither treats it as a delimiter either, so this is a beyond-upstream fix, not a missed port detail).

note: Real 2025 web release: SxxExx season+episode, split source (CR + WEB-DL), split audio (AAC + 2.0 + Dual-Audio), parenthetical alt-title dropped.

output.title: That Time I Got Reincarnated as a Slime

note: Minimal `[Group] Title - NN (res)` layout.

input: [Judas] Dr Stone - Science Future (Season 04 pt3) [1080p][HEVC x265 10bit][Dual-Audio][Multi-Subs] (Batch)
note: `pt3` part abbreviation (glued). Added 'Pt' as an ambiguous part-prefix keyword in detail/keyword.rs; it only counts enclosed, and the longer 'pt-BR' language keyword still wins by longest-match so it isn't mistaken for a part.

input: [Judas] Yuusha no Kuzu (Scum of the Brave) (Season 1 pt.2) [1080p][HEVC x265 10bit][Multi-Subs] (Batch)
note: `pt.2` part abbreviation (dot-separated); parenthetical English alt-title stays in the title, matching how the corpus handles `(alt name)` asides.

output.title: Yuusha no Kuzu (Scum of the Brave)

input: [HuangSubs] Martial Universe Season 4-6 (1080p) | (武动乾坤, Wu Dong Qiankun)
note: `Season 4-6` season range. detail/parser/season.rs's `Season N` branch now captures a trailing `-M` endpoint too (mirroring the existing `S01-02` handling), so both range ends become season values and the trailing number isn't left for the episode parser.

input: [neoDESU] Miss Kobayashi's Dragon Maid [Season 2 + Movie] [BD 1080p HEVC AV1 OPUS AAC] [Dual Audio] Kobayashi-san Chi no Maid Dragon
note: Bundle manifest: `[Season 2 + Movie]` packages a whole season plus a movie (verified against the torrent's file list: a `Season 2` folder of S02E01-E12 and a `Specials` folder). The `+` joining season/type content is the tell, so detail/parser.rs's detect_content_bundle flags `release_information: Batch`; the season and type are kept as the batch's contents (matching how upstream labels the SAO franchise bundle).

note: Season 0 (specials) with an `E01-E06` episode range and `Remux` release information.

input: [Gecko] Nonhuman - S03E23 (非人哉; Fei Ren Zai 3rd Season Part 2) [BILI.WEB-DL 1080P AVC, AAC, SUB][D402DAFE]
note: SxxExx plus a `Part 2` recovered from the CJK/romaji parenthetical; split `BILI.WEB-DL` source and trailing checksum.

input: [WonderhoyRips] BLEACH: Thousand-Year Blood War - The Calamity [E41 E42 E43] [720p SCR][H.264][AAC][EN Hardsub]
note: Explicit multi-episode batch `[E41 E42 E43]` (three episode values); colon kept in the title.

output.title: BLEACH: Thousand-Year Blood War - The Calamity

note: No episode or metadata present: the whole string is the title. `!!`, the ` - ` word separator, and the `+` are all kept rather than treated as delimiters that split off a phantom episode/episode_title.

`E06v1.5` is episode 6 at release version 1.5: the `.5` extends the version marker, not the episode number. Regression guard for a fix where the trailing-fraction path glued `.5` onto the episode and fabricated a half-episode `06.5`. `JapDub` is an audio term, `GerEngSub` a dual-language subtitle tag (not a release group).
Creditless-opening tag `NCOP` is split off as a type plus opening number `1a`, rather than left glued onto the end of the title (which is what upstream's develop parser does).

output.title: Jojo's Bizarre Adventure Diamond is Unbreakable

note: `Ver1.1a` is part of the real title (the game's version subtitle), not a release version or partial episode; the ` - 01` after it is the episode. See erengy/taiga#1110, erengy/anitomy#20.
Spaceless dash directly after `S01E12` must not swallow the following bracket group into the episode/title. See erengy/taiga#1222.
Leading `2.5` is part of the title ("2.5-dimensional"), not a fractional episode. `JapDub` is audio, `GerSub` subtitles.

input: NEET Kunoichi to Naze ka Dousei Hajimemashita E01-E02 [1080p][AAC][JapDub][GerSub][Web-DL].mkv
note: Dashed `E01-E02` range is parsed as two episodes (01 and 02), not episode 01 plus an episode_title of `E02`.

output.title: NEET Kunoichi to Naze ka Dousei Hajimemashita

note: Opaque hex hash filename: only a title and extension come out, with no hex run mis-read as a checksum or episode.

input: Ore wa Seikan Kokka no Akutoku Ryoushu! E1P [1080p][AAC][JapDub][GerEngSub][Web-DL].mkv
note: Malformed episode marker `E1P` (a typo for `EP1`) is left in the title rather than force-parsed into an episode. `JapDub` is audio, `GerEngSub` subtitles.

output.title: Ore wa Seikan Kokka no Akutoku Ryoushu! E1P

note: TARGET (issue #2, same as erengy/anitomy #36; audio-term half is issue #4). Bare range `17-26` is title text; marked `E01` is the episode (a marked episode blocks promoting a bare range). `GerJapDub` is a `<lang-code>+Dub` audio term, matched generically rather than as the release group.
Issue #4: multi-code dub/sub tags matched generically. `KorChiDub` is audio, the triple-code `GerEngJapSub` is subtitles — neither is enumerated as a keyword.
Adversarial guard for issue #4: a release group ending in `Subs` must not be reclassified as a subtitle tag — `horrible` isn't a run of three-letter language codes.
Adversarial guard for issue #4: `GerEn` is five letters, not whole three-letter codes, so `GerEnSub` is not a language tag and stays the (unenclosed-bracket) release group.
Guard: a bare numeric range with no marked episode is a genuine episode range and must stay one.
Guard: a marked range (SxxExx-Exx) stays an episode range; marker priority only suppresses bare, unmarked ranges.
Guard: a single stray number beside a marked E01 already stays in the title (only ranges are affected), so the fix must not over-suppress it.
